caption: This educational video presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on a "Tutorial – 3" regarding the "Work/unit mass of gas compressed." The screen displays a technical lecture slide featuring a thermodynamic diagram and a data table. The diagram plots properties with axes for "s" and "T," showing various process lines labeled "T=const," "p=const," and "s=const." Below the diagram is a table listing values for pressure "p (bar)," temperature "T (K)," enthalpy "h (j/g)," and entropy "s (j/gK)" for states "1" and "2." A mathematical formula for work, $\frac{W}{m} = T_1^*(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)$, is shown. Across the frames, the slide remains static, but a calculation is gradually completed at the bottom, resulting in the final answer: $\frac{W}{m} = 300(3.9-2.7) - (349-315) = 326 \text{ J/g}$. Logos for "NPTEL" are visible, and the footer attributes the material to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay."
